title: What Is Learning to Rank?
concept: Training a model specifically to order a list of candidates by relevance, rather than to classify or regress.

exercises.prompt: As we build LLM applications, how does ordering a list of items relate to showing the best AI response?

options.text: By generating candidate answers that need to be ordered by quality.
By replacing the LLM entirely with a simple lookup table.
By converting all text into random numbers.

exercises.explain: In LLM applications, we often generate many candidate responses and need a way to select or order the best ones for the user.

cards.title: Order Matters!
cards.body: When you search online, you don't just want relevant results; you want the absolute best results at the very top. Traditional models look at items one by one, but ranking models look at the whole list to put the best ones first.

cards.title: Defining Learning to Rank
cards.body: Learning to Rank (LTR) is a machine learning method where a model is trained specifically to sort a list of items. Instead of predicting a single score or class in isolation, it learns the best order for a group of candidates.

cards.title: The Librarian's Helper
cards.body: Imagine you are a librarian helper. If you look at books one by one and grade them 'good' or 'bad', that is classification. But if you gather all the magic books and line them up from the most magical to the least magical, you are ranking them as a group.

cards.title: Search Results in Action
cards.body: When you search for 'best pizza' on a map app, the system doesn't just tell you if a restaurant is open or closed. It uses ranking to list the closest, highest-rated pizza shops at the very top of your screen.

cards.title: It's Not Just Simple Sorting
cards.body: Many think ranking is just sorting items by a simple score like popularity. In reality, Learning to Rank models look at how items relate to each other and the user's search term to build the perfect custom list.

cards.title: Comparing vs. Grading
cards.body: Classification looks at items one by one in isolation, while Ranking looks at how items compare to each other to sort them.

left.text: Is this movie good or bad?

exercises.prompt: Which of the following tasks is the best fit for a Learning to Rank model?

options.text: It predicts whether a single image contains a cat or a dog.
It orders a list of candidate search results by relevance.
It calculates the exact price of a house based on square footage.
It groups customer emails into different support folders.

exercises.explain: Learning to Rank is unique because it evaluates candidates relative to one another to produce an ordered list, rather than classifying them or predicting an isolated number.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Classifying an item as 'relevant' or 'irrelevant' is the exact same thing as ranking a list.
exercises.explain: Classification categorizes items individually, whereas ranking sorts an entire list of items relative to one another.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the definition of Learning to Rank.
exercises.sentence: The main goal of Learning to Rank is to predict the correct order of a list of items.
exercises.explain: Learning to Rank is explicitly designed to predict the correct order of a list, rather than giving individual isolated scores.

exercises.prompt: Identify the search result ranking system powered by Learning to Rank.

options.text: A system that lists products in alphabetical order.
An AI ranker that dynamically re-orders documents based on user intent and relevance signals.
A website that displays articles in the exact order they were published.

exercises.explain: Option B is typical of an AI search engine using Learning to Rank to order candidates dynamically based on relevance, while A and C are basic static rule-based systems.

exercises.prompt: You want to build a search engine for a digital library using Learning to Rank. How does this system decide which books to show first when a user searches for 'space exploration'?

options.text: It predicts a separate 1-to-10 rating for each book individually.
It compares a set of candidate books to output the best ordered list.
It filters out books that contain specific banned words.
It randomly shuffles books to keep the search results fresh.

exercises.explain: Learning to Rank models look at the entire group of candidates at once to predict their relative order, rather than evaluating each item in absolute isolation. This ensures the best overall list is presented to the user.
